source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A beam of neutrons (mass $m$ ) traveling with nonrelativistic speed $v$ impinges on the system shown in Fig. 1.7, with beam-splitting mirrors at corners $B$ and $D$, mirrors at $A$ and $C$, and a neutron detector at $E$. The corners all make right angles, and neither the mirrors nor the beam-splitters affect the neutron spin. The beams separated at $B$ rejoin coherently at $D$, and the detector $E$ reports the neutron intensity $I$. For this part of the problem, suppose the system lies in a horizontal plane. A uniform magnetic field, pointing out of the plane, acts in the dotted region indicated which encompasses a portion of the leg BC. The incident neutrons are polarized with spin pointing along BA as shown. The neutrons which pass through the magnetic field region will have their spins pressed by an amount depending on the field strength. Suppose the spin expectation value presses through an angle $\theta$ as shown. Let $I(\theta)$ be the intensity at the detector $E$. Derive $I(\theta)$ as a function of $\theta$, given that $I(\theta=0)=I_{0}$.
extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL image object string rather than a concrete file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string rather than a concrete file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干显式引用“Fig. 1.7”并依赖装置中 A/B/C/D/E、BC 段和磁场虚线区域的位置关系。
requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，说明题目主要依赖图示而非纯文本。
需要根据两条路径在分束器/反射镜中的传播与重合关系来理解求解对象，单靠答案文本难以完整重建。
multi_step: 题目要求“derive I(theta)”而非直接读数，至少涉及路径分束-合束、磁场导致自旋转角、以及探测强度与态重叠/干涉的组合。
需要把空间路径信息与自旋态变化联合起来，属于图文结合的多阶段物理推导。
答案为解析式 I0 cos^2(theta/4)，表明中间通常要经过幅度或内积关系，不是单一步事实检索。
verifiability: answer_type=short_text，目标答案是明确的闭式表达式，便于规则或人工核验。
给定基准条件 I(theta=0)=I0，可快速检查候选表达式是否满足边界条件。

risk_flags: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string rather than a concrete file path or URL

初步评分显示图像依赖高、可核验性高，且答案为明确的短文本解析式，整体候选价值较高。
虽然 metadata.image_paths 存在记录格式问题，但 image_manifest 中已有可用的具体图片文件路径，问题不影响核心内容恢复。

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A beam of neutrons (mass $m$ ) traveling with nonrelativistic speed $v$ impinges on the system shown in Fig. 1.7, with beam-splitting mirrors at corners $B$ and $D$, mirrors at $A$ and $C$, and a neutron detector at $E$. The corners all make right angles, and neither the mirrors nor the beam-splitters affect the neutron spin. The beams separated at $B$ rejoin coherently at $D$, and the detector $E$ reports the neutron intensity $I$. For this part of the problem, suppose the system lies in a horizontal plane. A uniform magnetic field, pointing out of the plane, acts in the dotted region indicated which encompasses a portion of the leg BC. The incident neutrons are polarized with spin pointing along BA as shown. The neutrons which pass through the magnetic field region will have their spins pressed by an amount depending on the field strength. Suppose the spin expectation value presses through an angle $\theta$ as shown. Let $I(\theta)$ be the intensity at the detector $E$. Derive $I(\theta)$ as a function of $\theta$, given that $I(\theta=0)=I_{0}$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL image object string rather than a concrete file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain:
Take $z$-axis in the direction of BA and proceed in the representation of $\left(\mathbf{s}^{2}, s_{z}\right)$. At $D$ the spin state is $\binom{1}{0}$ for neutrons proceeding along BAD and is $\binom{\cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{\sin \frac{\theta}{2}}$ for those proceeding along BCD. Recombination gives
$$
\begin{aligned}
\Psi & =\frac{\sqrt{I_{0}}}{2} \exp \left(-i \frac{E t}{\hbar}\right) \exp (i \delta)\left[\binom{1}{0}+\binom{\cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{\sin \frac{\theta}{2}}\right] 
& =\frac{\sqrt{I_{0}}}{2} \exp \left(-i \frac{E t}{\hbar}\right) \exp (i \delta)\binom{1+\cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{\sin \frac{\theta}{2}}
\end{aligned}
$$
and hence
$$
I(\theta)=|\Psi|^{2}=\frac{I_{0}}{4}\left[\left(1+\cos \frac{\theta}{2}\right)^{2}+\sin ^{2} \frac{\theta}{2}\right]=I_{0} \cos ^{2} \frac{\theta}{4} .
$$

It remains semantically usable, so review is preferable to rejection.

conflict_pairs.detail: 文本中的视觉锚点明显多于可识别视觉区域。

field_audit_records.before_value: A beam of neutrons (mass $m$ ) traveling with nonrelativistic speed $v$ impinges on the system shown in Fig. 1.7, with beam-splitting mirrors at corners $B$ and $D$, mirrors at $A$ and $C$, and a neutron detector at $E$. The corners all make right angles, and neither the mirrors nor the beam-splitters affect the neutron spin. The beams separated at $B$ rejoin coherently at $D$, and the detector $E$ reports the neutron intensity $I$. For this part of the problem, suppose the system lies in a horizontal plane. A uniform magnetic field, pointing out of the plane, acts in the dotted region indicated which encompasses a portion of the leg BC. The incident neutrons are polarized with spin pointing along BA as shown. The neutrons which pass through the magnetic field region will have their spins pressed by an amount depending on the field strength. Suppose the spin expectation value presses through an angle $\theta$ as shown. Let $I(\theta)$ be the intensity at the detector $E$. Derive $I(\theta)$ as a function of $\theta$, given that $I(\theta=0)=I_{0}$.

The corners all make right angles, and neither the mirrors nor the beam-splitters affect the neutron spin. The beams separated at $B$ rejoin coherently at $D$, and the detector $E$ reports the neutron intensity $I$. For this part of the problem, suppose the system lies in a horizontal plane. A uniform magnetic field, pointing out of the plane, acts in the dotted region indicated which encompasses a portion of the leg BC. The incident neutrons are polarized with spin pointing along BA as shown. The neutrons which pass through the magnetic field region will have their spins pressed by an amount depending on the field strength. Derive $I(\theta)$ as a function of $\theta$, given that $I(\theta=0)=I_{0}$.
